codes.BI: Over 5 years and up to 10 years
codes.BK: Over 2 years and up to 5 years
codes.BL: Over 2 years and up to 3 years
codes.BM: Over 3 years and up to 4 years
codes.BN: Over 4 years and up to 5 years

codes.BQ: Over 7 years and up to 8 years
codes.BR: Over 8 years and up to 9 years
codes.BS: Over 9 years and up to 10 years
codes.BT: Over 3 years and up to 5 years
codes.LB: Over 1 week and up to 1 month
codes.LC: Over 1 month and up to 3 months
codes.LD: Over 3 months and up to 6 months
codes.LE: Over 6 months and up to 1 year
codes.LO: Over 3 months and up to 12 months
codes.OF: All forward OIS transactions except novations
codes.OS: All spot OIS transactions except novations
codes.KG: Forwards other than MP-dated, IMM-dated and FD12M24M
codes.LF: Over 6 months and up to 9 months
codes.LG: Over 9 months and up to 1 year
codes.LP: Over 1 week and up to 3 months
codes.NY15: Maturing in the first 15 calendar days of the calendar year following the trade date year

dimensions.name: Data type in money market context

codes.10: Question 1 - Percentage of banks that find the market not efficient
codes.11: Question 1 - Percentage of banks that find the market limitedly efficient
codes.12: Question 1 - Percentage of banks that find the market sufficiently efficient
codes.13: Question 1 - Percentage of banks that find the market significantly efficient
codes.14: Question 1 - Percentage of banks that find the market extremely efficient
codes.1Q: Question 1 - Is the euro money market (for the different segments) in your opinion efficient
codes.1Z: Question 1 - Percentage of banks with no opinion
codes.20: Question 2 - Percentage of banks that find the market liquidity has worsened significantly
codes.21: Question 2 - Percentage of banks that find the market liquidity has worsened slightly
codes.22: Question 2 - Percentage of banks that find the market liquidity has not changed
codes.23: Question 2 - Percentage of banks that find the market liquidity has improved slightly
codes.24: Question 2 - Percentage of banks that find the market liquidity has improved significantly
codes.2Q: Question 2 - Has the market liquidity in the euro money market changed with respect to last year
codes.2Z: Question 2 - Percentage of banks with no opinion
codes.3E: Question 3 - Percentage of the euro money market business (in nominal value) done with euro area counterparties (other than domestic)
codes.3N: Question 3 - Percentage of the euro money market business (in nominal value) done with national counterparties
codes.3O: Question 3 - Percentage of the euro money market business (in nominal value) done with counterparties other than euro area and national
codes.4D: Question 4 - Percentage of transactions with counterparties executed directly
codes.4E: Question 4 - Percentage of transactions with counterparties executed via electronic broker
codes.4V: Question 4 - Percentage of transactions with counterparties executed via voice broker
codes.50: Question 5 - Percentage of banks that find the market trading not changed
codes.52: Question 5 - Percentage of banks that find the market trading contracted
codes.53: Question 5 - Percentage of banks that find the market trading expanded
codes.5O: Question 5 - Percentage of transactions with counterparties executed at non-standard settlement date (Forward) (question up to 2011)
codes.5Q: Question 5 - Has your interbank money market trading changed during the reporting period because of changes in your risk limits
codes.5S: Question 5 - Percentage of transactions with counterparties executed at standard settlement date (Spot) (question up to 2011)
codes.60: Question 6 - Percentage of banks that find the market trading will not change
codes.62: Question 6 - Percentage of banks that find the market trading will contract
codes.63: Question 6 - Percentage of banks that find the market trading will expand
codes.6Q: Question 6 - Do you expect that your interbank money market trading will change because of foreseen changes in your risk limits
codes.A1: Index, aggregated average daily turnover volume in 2002 = 100
codes.AC: Percentage share in the average daily turnover in the total for all the banks from certain country
codes.AM: Average maturity (volume weighted) of the reported average daily turnover
codes.AP: Percentage share in the average daily turnover in the total for all the banks from the panel
codes.AS: Percentage share in the average daily turnover in the total for all the banks participating in the survey
codes.BD: Number of business days considered in the reporting period
codes.HT: Herfindahl index on average daily turnover
codes.IT: Index compiled from average daily turnover
codes.MP: Ratio of turnover times number of days in maturity bucket over average turnover times average maturity in the segment
codes.MW: Share of maturity bucket in market segment
codes.NE: Percentage of the reported average daily turnover not indexed on EONIA
codes.PF: Percentage of the reported average daily turnover indexed on floating rate (e.g. EONIA)
codes.PS: Number of participating banks in each segment
codes.SP: Spread between EONIA and Euro short-term rate
codes.TA: Number of banks which reported non-zero turnover
codes.TO: Share of turnover in currencies other than USD, CHF, JPY and GBP
codes.TS: Turnover shares - Contribution to overall segment turnover (U, R, D, S)
codes.TW: Share in total turnover (all markets combined)
codes.VL: Share of volume of the 5 largest active banks
codes.WS: Maturity weighted turnover shares - Contribution to overall segment turnover (U, R, D, S) (temporary value)
codes.SFXO: Spread between the EUR/USD FX swaps implied rate and the SOFR
codes.SFXT: Spread between the EUR/USD FX swaps implied rate and the SOFR OIS rate
codes.SY: Yield spread between higher and lower rated instruments and issuers
codes.SBL: Spread between lending and borrowing rate
codes.RC: Rate change after change of ECB policy rates
codes.DT: Difference in daily turnover between the last business day of the quarter and the first business day of the next quarter
codes.DR: Difference in weighted average rates between the last business day of the quarter and the first business day of the next quarter
codes.DA: Deviation in average daily turnover from the yearly average
